examples.title: Algorithm: Virtual Machine Lifecycle Management
examples.description: Demonstrates the lifecycle states of a VM from creation to termination
examples.code:
ALGORITHM VM_Lifecycle_Management
INPUT: VM request (name, cpu, memory, os_type)
OUTPUT: VM state transitions and resource allocation

BEGIN
  // Define VM states
  SET states = [CREATED, CONFIGURED, RUNNING, PAUSED, STOPPED, TERMINATED]
  
  // Function: Create VM
  FUNCTION Create_VM(name, cpu, memory, os_type)
    SET vm = NEW VM_Object
    vm.name = name
    vm.cpu = cpu
    vm.memory = memory
    vm.os_type = os_type
    vm.state = CREATED
    RETURN vm
  END FUNCTION
  
  // Function: Configure VM
  FUNCTION Configure_VM(vm, hypervisor)
    IF vm.state == CREATED THEN
      CALL Allocate_Resources(vm, hypervisor)
      vm.state = CONFIGURED
    END IF
    RETURN vm
  END FUNCTION
  
  // Function: Allocate Resources
  FUNCTION Allocate_Resources(vm, hypervisor)
    IF hypervisor.available_cpu >= vm.cpu AND hypervisor.available_memory >= vm.memory THEN
      hypervisor.available_cpu = hypervisor.available_cpu - vm.cpu
      hypervisor.available_memory = hypervisor.available_memory - vm.memory
      vm.hardware = NEW Virtual_Hardware(vm.cpu, vm.memory)
      RETURN TRUE
    ELSE
      RETURN FALSE  // Insufficient resources
    END IF
  END FUNCTION
  
  // Function: Start VM
  FUNCTION Start_VM(vm)
    IF vm.state == CONFIGURED OR vm.state == STOPPED THEN
      vm.state = RUNNING
      vm.start_time = CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
    END IF
    RETURN vm
  END FUNCTION
  
  // Function: Pause VM
  FUNCTION Pause_VM(vm)
    IF vm.state == RUNNING THEN
      vm.state = PAUSED
    END IF
    RETURN vm
  END FUNCTION
  
  // Function: Stop VM
  FUNCTION Stop_VM(vm)
    IF vm.state == RUNNING OR vm.state == PAUSED THEN
      vm.state = STOPPED
    END IF
    RETURN vm
  END FUNCTION
  
  // Function: Terminate VM
  FUNCTION Terminate_VM(vm, hypervisor)
    IF vm.state != TERMINATED THEN
      // Release resources back to hypervisor
      hypervisor.available_cpu = hypervisor.available_cpu + vm.cpu
      hypervisor.available_memory = hypervisor.available_memory + vm.memory
      vm.state = TERMINATED
    END IF
    RETURN vm
  END FUNCTION
  
  // Main execution example
  SET hypervisor = NEW Hypervisor(available_cpu=16, available_memory=32768)
  SET myVM = CALL Create_VM("WebServerVM", cpu=2, memory=4096, os_type="Linux")
  SET myVM = CALL Configure_VM(myVM, hypervisor)
  SET myVM = CALL Start_VM(myVM)
  SET myVM = CALL Pause_VM(myVM)
  SET myVM = CALL Stop_VM(myVM)
  SET myVM = CALL Terminate_VM(myVM, hypervisor)
END
examples.explanation: This pseudocode demonstrates the complete lifecycle of a virtual machine: CREATED → CONFIGURED → RUNNING → PAUSED/STOPPED → TERMINATED. Key operations include resource allocation from the hypervisor's pool, state transitions, and proper resource cleanup upon termination.

examples.title: Python: VM Resource Allocation Simulator
examples.description: Simulates resource allocation in a virtualized data center environment
examples.code:
"""
Virtual Machine Resource Allocation Simulator
Demonstrates how a hypervisor allocates CPU and memory resources to VMs
"""

import time
from enum import Enum
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from typing import List, Dict, Optional

class VMState(Enum):
    """Virtual Machine states in its lifecycle"""
    CREATED = "Created"
    CONFIGURED = "Configured"
    RUNNING = "Running"
    PAUSED = "Paused"
    STOPPED = "Stopped"
    TERMINATED = "Terminated"

@dataclass
class VirtualMachine:
    """Represents a Virtual Machine"""
    vm_id: str
    name: str
    os_type: str
    cpu_cores: int
    memory_mb: int
    state: VMState = VMState.CREATED
    start_time: Optional[float] = None
    
    def __str__(self):
        return f"VM {self.name}({self.vm_id}): {self.state.value} [CPU:{self.cpu_cores}, RAM:{self.memory_mb}MB]"

class Hypervisor:
    """
    Hypervisor that manages physical resources and allocates them to VMs
    """
    def __init__(self, total_cpu: int, total_memory_mb: int):
        self.total_cpu = total_cpu
        self.total_memory = total_memory_mb
        self.available_cpu = total_cpu
        self.available_memory = total_memory_mb
        self.vms: Dict[str, VirtualMachine] = {}
        
    def create_vm(self, name: str, os_type: str, cpu: int, memory: int) -> Optional[VirtualMachine]:
        """Create a new VM with specified resources"""
        vm_id = f"vm-{len(self.vms) + 1:03d}"
        vm = VirtualMachine(
            vm_id=vm_id,
            name=name,
            os_type=os_type,
            cpu_cores=cpu,
            memory_mb=memory
        )
        self.vms[vm_id] = vm
        print(f"✓ Created: {vm}")
        return vm
    
    def configure_vm(self, vm_id: str) -> bool:
        """Configure VM with virtual hardware"""
        vm = self.vms.get(vm_id)
        if not vm or vm.state != VMState.CREATED:
            print(f"✗ Cannot configure: VM {vm_id} not found or invalid state")
            return False
        
        vm.state = VMState.CONFIGURED
        print(f"✓ Configured: {vm}")
        return True
    
    def start_vm(self, vm_id: str) -> bool:
        """Start a VM by allocating resources"""
        vm = self.vms.get(vm_id)
        if not vm or vm.state not in [VMState.CONFIGURED, VMState.STOPPED]:
            print(f"✗ Cannot start: VM {vm_id} not found or invalid state")
            return False
        
        # Check resource availability
        if vm.cpu_cores > self.available_cpu or vm.memory_mb > self.available_memory:
            print(f"✗ Insufficient resources to start {vm.name}")
            return False
        
        # Allocate resources
        self.available_cpu -= vm.cpu_cores
        self.available_memory -= vm.memory_mb
        vm.state = VMState.RUNNING
        vm.start_time = time.time()
        
        print(f"✓ Started: {vm}")
        print(f"  Resources: CPU {self.available_cpu}/{self.total_cpu}, RAM {self.available_memory}/{self.total_memory}MB")
        return True
    
    def stop_vm(self, vm_id: str) -> bool:
        """Stop a VM and release its resources"""
        vm = self.vms.get(vm_id)
        if not vm or vm.state != VMState.RUNNING:
            print(f"✗ Cannot stop: VM {vm_id} not running")
            return False
        
        # Release resources
        self.available_cpu += vm.cpu_cores
        self.available_memory += vm.memory_mb
        vm.state = VMState.STOPPED
        
        print(f"✓ Stopped: {vm}")
        print(f"  Resources: CPU {self.available_cpu}/{self.total_cpu}, RAM {self.available_memory}/{self.total_memory}MB")
        return True
    
    def terminate_vm(self, vm_id: str) -> bool:
        """Terminate and remove a VM"""
        vm = self.vms.get(vm_id)
        if not vm:
            print(f"✗ VM {vm_id} not found")
            return False
        
        if vm.state == VMState.RUNNING:
            # Stop first to release resources
            self.stop_vm(vm_id)
        
        vm.state = VMState.TERMINATED
        print(f"✓ Terminated: {vm}")
        del self.vms[vm_id]
        return True
    
    def get_status(self) -> None:
        """Display hypervisor status"""
        print("\n" + "="*60)
        print("HYPERVISOR STATUS")
        print("="*60)
        print(f"Total Resources: CPU={self.total_cpu} cores, RAM={self.total_memory}MB")
        print(f"Available:       CPU={self.available_cpu} cores, RAM={self.available_memory}MB")
        print(f"Active VMs: {len([v for v in self.vms.values() if v.state != VMState.TERMINATED])}")
        print("-"*60)
        for vm in self.vms.values():
            print(f"  {vm}")
        print("="*60 + "\n")

# Demo execution
if __name__ == "__main__":
    # Create hypervisor with 16 CPU cores and 32GB RAM
    hypervisor = Hypervisor(total_cpu=16, total_memory_mb=32768)
    
    # Create multiple VMs
    vm1 = hypervisor.create_vm("WebServer", "Ubuntu Linux", cpu=2, memory=4096)
    vm2 = hypervisor.create_vm("DatabaseServer", "CentOS", cpu=4, memory=8192)
    vm3 = hypervisor.create_vm("DevEnvironment", "Windows Server", cpu=2, memory=4096)
    vm4 = hypervisor.create_vm("AppServer", "Ubuntu Linux", cpu=4, memory=8192)
    
    # Configure and start VMs
    for vm_id in ["vm-0001", "vm-0002", "vm-0003"]:
        hypervisor.configure_vm(vm_id)
        hypervisor.start_vm(vm_id)
    
    hypervisor.get_status()
    
    # Try to start 4th VM (may fail due to insufficient resources)
    hypervisor.configure_vm("vm-0004")
    hypervisor.start_vm("vm-0004")
    
    hypervisor.get_status()
    
    # Stop and terminate a VM to free resources
    hypervisor.stop_vm("vm-0001")
    hypervisor.get_status()
    
    # Now start the 4th VM
    hypervisor.start_vm("vm-0004")
    hypervisor.get_status()
examples.explanation: This Python implementation simulates a hypervisor managing physical resources (CPU cores and memory) and allocating them to virtual machines. It demonstrates VM lifecycle management, resource allocation/deallocation, resource checking before VM startup, and resource pooling. The example shows how virtualization enables hardware consolidation by running multiple VMs on shared physical resources.
examples.timeComplexity: O(1) for VM operations, O(n) for status display with n VMs
examples.spaceComplexity: O(n) where n is the number of VMs

examples.title: C: Hypervisor Resource Manager
examples.description: C implementation showing hypervisor resource management and VM scheduling
examples.explanation: This C implementation demonstrates a hypervisor managing physical resources and virtual machines. Key features include: dynamic VM creation, resource allocation tracking, state machine management (Created→Configured→Running→Stopped→Terminated), resource validation before VM startup, proper cleanup on termination, and demonstration of hardware consolidation through resource pooling. The example shows how a single physical host can run multiple VMs by sharing CPU and memory resources.
examples.timeComplexity: O(1) for VM operations, O(n) for status display with n VMs
examples.spaceComplexity: O(n) where n is the number of VMs (max 100)

examples.title: Python: Cluster Virtualization Scheduler
examples.description: Demonstrates cluster resource scheduling and VM placement in data center virtualization
examples.code:
"""
Cluster Virtualization Scheduler
Simulates VM placement across a cluster of physical servers in a data center
"""

import random
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from typing import List, Dict, Optional
from enum import Enum

class VMState(Enum):
    PENDING = "Pending"
    RUNNING = "Running"
    FAILED = "Failed"

@dataclass
class VirtualMachine:
    """Represents a VM request"""
    vm_id: str
    name: str
    cpu_required: int
    memory_required: int  # in GB
    state: VMState = VMState.PENDING
    assigned_node: Optional[str] = None

@dataclass
class PhysicalNode:
    """Represents a physical server in the cluster"""
    node_id: str
    total_cpu: int
    total_memory: int  # in GB
    cpu_available: int = 0
    memory_available: int = 0
    vms: List[VirtualMachine] = field(default_factory=list)
    
    def __post_init__(self):
        self.cpu_available = self.total_cpu
        self.memory_available = self.total_memory
    
    def can_fit(self, vm: VirtualMachine) -> bool:
        """Check if node has enough resources for the VM"""
        return (self.cpu_available >= vm.cpu_required and 
                self.memory_available >= vm.memory_required)
    
    def allocate_vm(self, vm: VirtualMachine) -> bool:
        """Allocate resources to a VM"""
        if not self.can_fit(vm):
            return False
        self.cpu_available -= vm.cpu_required
        self.memory_available -= vm.memory_required
        self.vms.append(vm)
        return True
    
    def release_vm(self, vm: VirtualMachine) -> bool:
        """Release VM resources"""
        if vm in self.vms:
            self.vms.remove(vm)
            self.cpu_available += vm.cpu_required
            self.memory_available += vm.memory_required
            return True
        return False
    
    def utilization(self) -> Dict[str, float]:
        """Calculate resource utilization percentage"""
        cpu_util = ((self.total_cpu - self.cpu_available) / self.total_cpu) * 100
        mem_util = ((self.total_memory - self.memory_available) / self.total_memory) * 100
        return {"cpu": cpu_util, "memory": mem_util}

class ClusterScheduler:
    """
    Manages VM placement across a cluster of physical nodes
    Demonstrates data center virtualization concepts
    """
    def __init__(self, nodes: List[PhysicalNode]):
        self.nodes = nodes
        self.vm_registry: Dict[str, VirtualMachine] = {}
    
    def add_vm(self, vm: VirtualMachine, strategy: str = "best_fit") -> bool:
        """Place VM on appropriate node using specified strategy"""
        self.vm_registry[vm.vm_id] = vm
        
        if strategy == "first_fit":
            return self._first_fit(vm)
        elif strategy == "best_fit":
            return self._best_fit(vm)
        elif strategy == "worst_fit":
            return self._worst_fit(vm)
        else:
            return self._first_fit(vm)
    
    def _first_fit(self, vm: VirtualMachine) -> bool:
        """Place VM on first node with sufficient resources"""
        for node in self.nodes:
            if node.can_fit(vm):
                node.allocate_vm(vm)
                vm.state = VMState.RUNNING
                vm.assigned_node = node.node_id
                return True
        vm.state = VMState.FAILED
        return False
    
    def _best_fit(self, vm: VirtualMachine) -> bool:
        """Place VM on node with smallest sufficient resources (minimizes waste)"""
        candidates = [n for n in self.nodes if n.can_fit(vm)]
        
        if not candidates:
            vm.state = VMState.FAILED
            return False
        
        # Find node with minimum wasted resources
        best_node = min(candidates, 
                       key=lambda n: (n.cpu_available - vm.cpu_required) + 
                                    (n.memory_available - vm.memory_required))
        
        best_node.allocate_vm(vm)
        vm.state = VMState.RUNNING
        vm.assigned_node = best_node.node_id
        return True
    
    def _worst_fit(self, vm: VirtualMachine) -> bool:
        """Place VM on node with most resources (maximizes even distribution)"""
        candidates = [n for n in self.nodes if n.can_fit(vm)]
        
        if not candidates:
            vm.state = VMState.FAILED
            return False
        
        # Find node with maximum available resources
        worst_node = max(candidates,
                         key=lambda n: n.cpu_available + n.memory_available)
        
        worst_node.allocate_vm(vm)
        vm.state = VMState.RUNNING
        vm.assigned_node = worst_node.node_id
        return False
    
    def migrate_vm(self, vm_id: str, target_node_id: str) -> bool:
        """Migrate running VM to another node"""
        vm = self.vm_registry.get(vm_id)
        if not vm or vm.state != VMState.RUNNING:
            return False
        
        # Find source and target nodes
        source_node = None
        target_node = None
        for node in self.nodes:
            if vm in node.vms:
                source_node = node
            if node.node_id == target_node_id:
                target_node = node
        
        if not source_node or not target_node:
            return False
        
        if not target_node.can_fit(vm):
            return False
        
        # Perform migration
        source_node.release_vm(vm)
        target_node.allocate_vm(vm)
        vm.assigned_node = target_node_id
        return True
    
    def get_cluster_status(self) -> None:
        """Display cluster-wide status"""
        print("\n" + "="*70)
        print("DATA CENTER CLUSTER STATUS")
        print("="*70)
        
        total_cpu = sum(n.total_cpu for n in self.nodes)
        total_mem = sum(n.total_memory for n in self.nodes)
        avail_cpu = sum(n.cpu_available for n in self.nodes)
        avail_mem = sum(n.memory_available for n in self.nodes)
        
        print(f"Cluster Resources: CPU={total_cpu} cores, RAM={total_mem}GB")
        print(f"Available:         CPU={avail_cpu} cores, RAM={avail_mem}GB")
        print(f"Utilization:       CPU={((total_cpu-avail_cpu)/total_cpu)*100:.1f}%, RAM={((total_mem-avail_mem)/total_mem)*100:.1f}%")
        print("-"*70)
        
        for node in self.nodes:
            util = node.utilization()
            print(f"Node {node.node_id}: CPU {util['cpu']:.1f}% | MEM {util['memory']:.1f}% | VMs: {len(node.vms)}")
            for vm in node.vms:
                print(f"  → {vm.name} (ID:{vm.vm_id}) CPU:{vm.cpu_required} RAM:{vm.memory_required}GB")
        
        print("="*70 + "\n")

# Demo execution
if __name__ == "__main__":
    # Create a data center cluster with 4 physical servers
    nodes = [
        PhysicalNode("node-01", total_cpu=32, total_memory=128),
        PhysicalNode("node-02", total_cpu=32, total_memory=128),
        PhysicalNode("node-03", total_cpu=64, total_memory=256),
        PhysicalNode("node-04", total_cpu=64, total_memory=256),
    ]
    
    scheduler = ClusterScheduler(nodes)
    
    print("=== Cluster Virtualization Scheduler Demo ===")
    
    # Create VM requests
    vms_to_create = [
        ("web-001", "WebServer-1", 4, 16),
        ("web-002", "WebServer-2", 4, 16),
        ("db-001", "Database-Primary", 8, 32),
        ("db-002", "Database-Replica", 8, 32),
        ("app-001", "AppServer-1", 6, 24),
        ("app-002", "AppServer-2", 6, 24),
        ("app-003", "AppServer-3", 6, 24),
        ("cache-001", "RedisCache", 2, 8),
        ("cache-002", "MemCache", 2, 8),
    ]
    
    # Add VMs using Best Fit strategy
    print("--- Deploying VMs (Best Fit Strategy) ---\n")
    for vm_id, name, cpu, mem in vms_to_create:
        vm = VirtualMachine(vm_id, name, cpu, mem)
        success = scheduler.add_vm(vm, strategy="best_fit")
        status = "✓ Deployed" if success else "✗ Failed"\n        node_info = f"on {vm.assigned_node}" if vm.assigned_node else ""
        print(f"{status}: {name} {node_info}")
    
    scheduler.get_cluster_status()
    
    # Demonstrate VM migration
    print("--- VM Migration Demo ---\n")
    print("Migrating cache-001 from node-01 to node-04...")
    success = scheduler.migrate_vm("cache-001", "node-04")
    print(f"Migration {'successful' if success else 'failed'}\n")
    
    scheduler.get_cluster_status()
examples.explanation: This Python implementation demonstrates cluster and data center virtualization concepts including: multi-node physical cluster management, VM placement algorithms (First Fit, Best Fit, Worst Fit), resource utilization tracking per node, live VM migration between nodes, cluster-wide resource aggregation, and load distribution strategies. This simulates how modern data centers virtualize physical resources across multiple servers to achieve high availability and resource efficiency.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) for VM placement, O(n) for migration where n is number of nodes
examples.spaceComplexity: O(n + m) where n is nodes and m is VMs
